name: wifite
title: wifite
summary: wifite - Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools

commands.name: wifite
commands.description: Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools

arguments.description: target a specific access point by bssid (mac) [auto]
arguments.raw: -b  target a specific access point by bssid (mac) [auto]

arguments.description: crack WPA handshakes using <dic> wordlist file [off]
arguments.raw: -crack  crack WPA handshakes using <dic> wordlist file [off]

arguments.description: specify dictionary to use when cracking WPA [phpbb.txt]
arguments.raw: -dict  specify dictionary to use when cracking WPA [phpbb.txt]

arguments.description: target a specific access point by ssid (name) [ask]
arguments.raw: -e  target a specific access point by ssid (name) [ask]

arguments.description: Changes MAC address of 'iface' to a random MAC. [off]
arguments.raw: -mac  Changes MAC address of 'iface' to a random MAC. [off]

arguments.description: stop attack if fake authentication fails [off]
arguments.raw: -nofakeauth  stop attack if fake authentication fails [off]

arguments.description: set the number of packets per second to inject [600]
arguments.raw: -pps  set the number of packets per second to inject [600]

arguments.description: strip handshake using tshark or pyrit [off]
arguments.raw: -strip  strip handshake using tshark or pyrit [off]

arguments.description: start cracking when number of ivs surpass n [10000]
arguments.raw: -wepca  start cracking when number of ivs surpass n [10000]

arguments.description: save a copy of .cap files to this directory [off]
arguments.raw: -wepsave  save a copy of .cap files to this directory [off]

arguments.description: sec to wait for each attack, 0 implies endless [600]
arguments.raw: -wept  sec to wait for each attack, 0 implies endless [600]

arguments.description: time to wait between sending deauth packets (sec) [10]
arguments.raw: -wpadt  time to wait between sending deauth packets (sec) [10]

arguments.description: time to wait for WPA attack to complete (seconds) [500]
arguments.raw: -wpat  time to wait for WPA attack to complete (seconds) [500]

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wifite -h
  .               .    
.´  ·  .     .  ·  `.  wifite2 2.8.1
:  :  :  (¯)  :  :  :  a wireless auditor by derv82
`.  ·  ` /¯\ ´  ·  .´  maintained by kimocoder
  `     /¯¯¯\     ´    https://github.com/kimocoder/wifite2
 
 options:
 -h, --help                    show this help message and exit
 
 SETTINGS:
 -v, --verbose                 Shows more options (-h -v). Prints commands and outputs. (default: quiet)
 -i [interface]                Wireless interface to use, e.g. wlan0mon (default: ask)
 -c [channel]                  Wireless channel to scan e.g. 1,3-6 (default: all 2Ghz channels)
 -inf, --infinite              Enable infinite attack mode. Modify scanning time with -p (default: off)
 -mac, --random-mac            Randomize wireless card MAC address (default: off)
 -p [scan_time]                Pillage: Attack all targets after scan_time (seconds)
 --kill                        Kill processes that conflict with Airmon/Airodump (default: off)
 -pow, --power [min_power]     Attacks any targets with at least min_power signal strength
 --skip-crack                  Skip cracking captured handshakes/pmkid (default: off)
 -first, --first [attack_max]  Attacks the first attack_max targets
 -ic, --ignore-cracked         Hides previously-cracked targets. (default: off)
 --clients-only                Only show targets that have associated clients (default: off)
 --nodeauths                   Passive mode: Never deauthenticates clients (default: deauth targets)
 --daemon                      Puts device back in managed mode after quitting (default: off)
 
 WEP:
 --wep                         Show only WEP-encrypted networks
 --require-fakeauth            Fails attacks if fake-auth fails (default: off)
 --keep-ivs                    Retain .IVS files and reuse when cracking (default: off)
 
 WPA:
 --wpa                         Show only WPA/WPA2-encrypted networks (may include WPS)
 --wpa3                        Show only WPA3-encrypted networks (SAE/OWE)
 --owe                         Show only OWE-encrypted networks (Enhanced Open)
 --new-hs                      Captures new handshakes, ignores existing handshakes in hs (default: off)
 --dict [file]                 File containing passwords for cracking (default: /usr/share/dict/wordlist-probable.txt)
 
 WPS:
 --wps                         Show only WPS-enabled networks
 --wps-only                    Only use WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: off)
 --bully                       Use bully program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
 --reaver                      Use reaver program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
 --ignore-locks                Do not stop WPS PIN attack if AP becomes locked (default: stop)
 
 PMKID:
 --pmkid                       Only use PMKID capture, avoids other WPS & WPA attacks (default: off)
 --no-pmkid                    Don't use PMKID capture (default: off)
 --pmkid-timeout [sec]         Time to wait for PMKID capture (default: 300 seconds)
 
 COMMANDS:
 --cracked                     Print previously-cracked access points
 --ignored                     Print ignored access points
 --check [file]                Check a .cap file (or all hs/*.cap files) for WPA handshakes
 --crack                       Show commands to crack a captured handshake
 --update-db                   Update the local MAC address prefix database from IEEE registries

examples.command:
wifite -pow 50 -wps

  .;'                     `;,
 .;'  ,;'             `;,  `;,   WiFite v2 (r85)
.;'  ,;'  ,;'     `;,  `;,  `;,
::   ::   :   ( )   :   ::   ::  automated wireless auditor
':.  ':.  ':. /_\ ,:'  ,:'  ,:'
 ':.  ':.    /___\    ,:'  ,:'   designed for Linux
  ':.       /_____\      ,:'
           /       \

 [+] targeting WPS-enabled networks

 [+] scanning for wireless devices...
 [+] enabling monitor mode on wlan0... done
 [+] initializing scan (mon0), updates at 5 sec intervals, CTRL+C when ready.

apt_metadata.depends: python3:any, aircrack-ng, reaver, tshark, net-tools, ieee-data, python3-chardet
apt_metadata.description:
Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools
Wifite is a tool to audit WEP or WPA encrypted wireless networks.
It uses aircrack-ng, pyrit, reaver, tshark tools to perform the audit.
.
This tool is customizable to be automated with only a few arguments
and can be trusted to run without supervision.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use wifite because wifite - Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wifite [SETTINGS] [FILTERS]` as documented upstream for wifite.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./wifite.py -wps -wep -c 6 -pps 600` as documented upstream for wifite.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair wifite with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wifite --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install wifite. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./wifite.py -wps -wep -c 6 -pps 600`. wifite - Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: wifite - Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: wifite - Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools.
